title: alchemilla alpestris subsp. obtusa
intro: Alchemilla alpestris subsp. obtusa, also known as the mountain lady's mantle, is a sub-species of the alpine lady's mantle plant, which is a perennial flowering plant native to mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which are due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla alpestris subsp. obtusa have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing swelling and soothing irritated skin, as well as acting as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment, and has been used in herbal remedies to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues.
